compositional_truth_check.details: P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not visible as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) remains with the rabbit anchor.

inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No visible silver guest foliage)

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only soil; no botanical growth detected.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Black pot contains only soil and eggshell debris; no mint or silver guest foliage detected.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen shows a single, chlorotic/pale leaf. Structural integrity is poor.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, moist, potentially over-saturated. No fungal blooms, but significant debris.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of a small electronic component (header pin strip) introduced as an anomaly.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a rapid decline of all specimens. P1 and P2/P4 have transitioned from potential growth to bare soil. P3 has lost all but one leaf, which is showing signs of severe stress/etiolation.

visual_health_inference.P3: Critical. The single leaf is pale, indicating lack of photosynthesis or severe nutrient/light stress. The plant is likely in a terminal state.
visual_health_inference.P1_P2_P4: Deceased/Lost. No viable plant matter remains.

anomalies: Introduction of a multi-pin electronic connector on the desk surface.
Presence of crushed eggshells in P3/P4 soil, likely an amateur attempt at calcium supplementation.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of collapse. The registry-listed plants (P1, P2, P4) are no longer present as living specimens. P3 is the sole survivor, exhibiting extreme chlorosis and structural weakness. The environment appears to be suffering from poor light management and potential over-watering, leading to the loss of the majority of the collection.
